title: euphrasia curta subsp. coerulea
intro: Euphrasia curta subsp. coerulea, also known as the alpine harebell or blue-flowering eyebright, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating eye and respiratory problems, such as conjunctivitis and bronchitis. The health benefits of Euphrasia curta subsp. coerulea include soothing irritated eyes and mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, and relieving coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
